title: penstemon × macewanii
intro: Penstemon × macewanii, also known as Macewan's penstemon or Macewan's beardtongue, is a hybrid plant species within the Penstemon genus. It is a perennial plant native to the western provinces of Canada, known for its showy, tubular flowers that attract pollinators. The medicinal properties of Penstemon × macewanii include its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful in treating wounds and reducing pain. Traditional herbal preparations of Penstemon × macewanii include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
